_description: Longitudes de decorrelación espacial por variable climática y reglas para variables no estocásticas
_rule: Cobertura espacial usando decaimiento exponencial exp(-d/L). Cada variable climática tiene L basado en literatura revisada por pares.
_status: MVP approximation — valores globales no calibrados regionalmente
_calibration: Pendiente: validación empírica con estaciones SENAMHI para Perú

_spatial_coverage_model._description: Distancias máximas derivadas del modelo de correlación espacial exponencial, no valores manuales.
_spatial_coverage_model.theta_justification: θ=0.5 representa la distancia donde la correlación espacial cae al 50%. Es el punto de referencia estándar en análisis geoestadístico de variogramas (Isaaks & Srivastava, 1989, 'An Introduction to Applied Geostatistics', Oxford University Press; Journel & Huijbregts, 1978, 'Mining Geostatistics', Academic Press). A ρ=0.5, el campo retiene suficiente señal para ser representativo del punto de consulta. Valores inferiores implican que el dato tiene más ruido que señal espacial.
_spatial_coverage_model.non_spatial_sources: Fuentes país-nivel (World Bank), índices de cuenca (ONI), y datos estáticos con resolution_ratio (elevación, población, edificaciones) no usan este modelo. Se marcan con max_distance_km=null.

reference.citation: Jones, P.D., T.J. Osborn, and K.R. Briffa (1997). J. Climate, 10, 2548–2568.
reference.scope: global mean for mid-latitude annual temperature
reference.caveat: Andean topography likely reduces decorrelation to 200–300 km

air_temperature_current.notes: Jones et al. (1997) zonally averaged correlation decay. Mid-latitudes ~500–1000 km. 500 km conservative for tropics.

reference.citation: Jones, P.D., T.J. Osborn, and K.R. Briffa (1997). J. Climate, 10, 2548–2568.
reference.scope: global mean — same decorrelation as mean temperature

reference.citation: Jones, P.D., T.J. Osborn, and K.R. Briffa (1997). J. Climate, 10, 2548–2568.
reference.scope: global mean — same decorrelation as mean temperature

reference.citation: Huffman, G.J. et al. (2001). J. Hydrometeor., 2(1), 36–50; Yang et al. (2015) Sichuan station density study
reference.caveat: Convective precipitation in tropical Andes has very short decorrelation (~10–30 km). Stratiform events may reach 50 km.

precipitation_current.notes: Yang et al. (2015): max admissible station spacing in complex Sichuan terrain = 16–42 km. GPCP 1DD at 1° (~110 km) does not resolve sub-grid variability.

reference.citation: Huffman, G.J. et al. (2001). J. Hydrometeor., 2(1), 36–50.
reference.scope: global mean for daily accumulated precipitation

precipitation_sum.notes: Accumulated precipitation inherits same decorrelation as instantaneous — accumulation period does not significantly change spatial structure at daily scale.

reference.citation: New, M. et al. (2002). Climate Research, 21, 1–25; Brotzge & Richardson (2003) OK Mesonet study
reference.scope: global mean for monthly relative humidity climatology
reference.caveat: OK Mesonet: ρ ≥ 0.88 at 100 km for RH. Andean valleys may have shorter decorrelation due to localized moisture sources.

reference.citation: Bandyopadhyay, S. et al. (2015). Environ. Res. Lett., 10, 044004.
reference.scope: global mean — integral length scales from CAN (273 km), AUS (368 km), BPA (89 km)
reference.caveat: Andean complex terrain: channeling effects create sharp gradients at ~50–100 km. MVP 200 km is optimistic for Peru.

reference.citation: Thorndike, A.S. (1982). J. Atmos. Sci., 39, 2229–2238; Madaus & Hakim (2013) AMS
reference.scope: global mean for synoptic-scale surface pressure
reference.caveat: Pressure is a synoptic-scale field integrating the full atmospheric column. 500 km is conservative — actual synoptic L may be >1000 km.

surface_pressure.notes: Thorndike (1982): Arctic surface pressure ~1000 km. METAR network at ~100 km spacing is redundant for synoptic pressure field. Surface pressure has the longest decorrelation of any meteorological variable.

reference.scope: GRACE native resolution — Gaussian smoothing radius
reference.caveat: GRACE post-processing smoothing radius IS the decorrelation length. DDK1~530 km, DDK2~340 km, DDK3~240 km. 300 km is standard.

twsa.notes: GRACE gravity field is smoothed to ~300 km by construction (Gaussian kernel). TWSA signals are not resolved below this scale. DDK filtering variants confirm the range.

elevation.description: DEM is a fixed topographic field, not a stochastic climate variable. Coverage based on DEM resolution vs required resolution.

reference.citation: Farr, T.G. et al. (2007). Rev. Geophys., 45(2), RG2004.

population.description: Socioeconomic gridded data. Coverage depends on native grid resolution vs required resolution.

buildings.description: Infrastructure density grid. Coverage depends on native grid resolution vs required resolution.

land_cover.description: Categorical classification. Coverage is 1.0 if source covers the pixel within its classification period, else null.

traveltime_healthcare.description: Accessibility raster. Coverage depends on native resolution vs required resolution.

oni_index.description: ONI is a single basin-scale index (Niño 3.4), not a gridded field. Spatial coverage is always 1.0 within tropical Pacific domain.

reference.citation: Trenberth, K.E. (1997). Bull. Amer. Meteor. Soc., 78, 2771–2777.

poverty_rate.description: Country-level socioeconomic indicator. Spatial coverage is 1.0 at national scale.

gdp_per_capita.description: Country-level socioeconomic indicator. Spatial coverage is 1.0 at national scale.

water_access.description: Country-level socioeconomic indicator. Spatial coverage is 1.0 at national scale.

urban_population.description: Country-level socioeconomic indicator. Spatial coverage is 1.0 at national scale.
